mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns exceeds +0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based economic demand that lifts corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC/BIC) over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production indicates immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced economic activity, causing short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of industrial production contraction periods, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, borrowing costs for corporations and households become restrictive enough to suppress capital expenditure and equity risk appetite, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates robust labor demand without wage-price spirals, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with increased corporate earnings and dividend payouts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in <45% of high-GDP-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 102, capacity utilization approaches peak levels, triggering inflationary expectations and central bank tightening fears that compress equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 102, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of stable inflation expectations.
